name: subfinder (Fast Passive Subdomain Discovery)

auth.notes: Optional API keys in ~/.config/subfinder/provider-config.yaml for premium sources

performance.notes: Passive only — fast (no active DNS brute-forcing)

agent_readiness.notes: Fast passive subdomain discovery from ProjectDiscovery. subfinder -d example.com. subfinder -dL domains.txt (list of targets). -o output.txt. JSON: -oJ. Multiple passive sources: CertSpotter, Censys, Shodan, VirusTotal, Chaos, HackerTarget, etc. Part of ProjectDiscovery toolkit (pairs with httpx, nuclei, katana). subfinder | httpx | nuclei pipeline for automated bug bounty. -all flag uses all sources.

gotchas: Passive only — misses subdomains not in passive sources
API keys required for best results (Censys, Shodan, Chaos)
No active brute-forcing (combine with gobuster DNS for that)
Rate limits from data sources can slow results
best_when: Fast passive subdomain enumeration for bug bounty
avoid_when: Comprehensive active discovery (amass has more sources + brute-force)
When CT log search alone suffices
summary: subfinder is ProjectDiscovery's fast passive subdomain discovery tool. Queries 40+ sources (Censys, Shodan, VirusTotal, CertSpotter, etc.). subfinder -d target.com. JSON output for pipelines. Part of the standard ProjectDiscovery toolkit: subfinder | httpx | nuclei. Faster than amass for passive-only discovery. API keys improve result coverage dramatically.
